items.title: Trump administration gives up legal battle to impose executive order on 4 law firms
items.summary_auto: The U.S. Justice Department has dropped a legal bid to revive President Donald Trump's executive orders targeting four prominent law firms over their past ‌legal work, diversity policies and political ties.

items.title: A timeline of how B.C. got to the decision to stop changing its clocks
Why Trump still needs to sell Iran war to U.S. voters
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump has given Americans a short list of his objectives in attacking Iran, but when it comes to the justification for launching the war and how the conflict is expected to play out, he and his team are sending mixed messages.

items.title: Carney lands in Australia looking to draw closer to a Commonwealth cousin also grappling with Trump
items.summary_auto: Prime Minister Mark Carney has landed in Australia as he looks to bolster ties with a Commonwealth cousin that is increasingly viewed in Ottawa as a key ally on global challenges — a country that has some newfound importance for Canada amid a lot of uncertainty elsewhere.

items.title: White nationalist fight clubs pose risk for 'extreme violence,' warns government report
items.summary_auto: The growing white nationalist "active club" movement poses a risk for "extreme violence" in Canada, according to an internal Public Safety Canada brief obtained by CBC News.

items.title: Killer whale moms struggle to regain body fat with more offspring
items.summary_auto: New Canadian-led research into northern resident killer whales suggests even when food is plentiful, orca moms struggle to regain body fat when they have more living offspring.

items.title: Your teen is likely seeing 'looksmaxxing' content online. Here's what you need to know
items.summary_auto: Here’s what parents and other concerned adults need to know about looksmaxxing, and how to talk about it with the youth.

items.title: Africa's record solar buildout includes tiny rural rooftops — and these Canadian companies
items.summary_auto: Some Canadian companies are using creative ideas and solar technology to power up rural Africa, from battery swapping to rent-to-own rooftop solar. They say the continent is full of opportunities to make money and make a difference.

items.title: Owner plans to reopen Ottawa parking garage a year after it partially collapsed. They still don't know what caused it
items.summary_auto: The owner of the downtown Ottawa parking garage where a large section came crashing down just over a year ago says it plans to rebuild and reopen the facility — though it remains unclear what caused the collapse, and the company is not sharing details about past inspections of the property.

items.title: An Ottawa parking garage partially collapsed a year ago and no one knows why. The owner plans to reopen
items.summary_auto: The owner of the downtown Ottawa parking garage where a large section came crashing down just over a year ago says it plans to rebuild and reopen the facility — though it remains unclear what caused the collapse, and the company is not sharing details about past inspections of the property.

items.title: What to know about Mideast air travel as widening war in Iran grounds flights
items.summary_auto: Air travel in the Middle East appears to be resuming after being suspended since Saturday amid airstrikes on Iran by the U.S. and Israel and the Islamic Republic's retaliatory attacks on other countries in the region.

items.title: Calgary boy, 8, endured 'torture,' court hears at father's child abuse sentencing hearing
items.summary_auto: A case of horrific child abuse was in a Calgary courtroom Monday as Justice Ken McLeod considers a fit sentence for a 41-year-old man who pleaded guilty last spring to aggravated assault, forcible confinement and failing to provide the necessaries of life.

items.title: Alberta Premier Danielle Smith looking to ditch clock changes as B.C. moves to permanent daylight
items.summary_auto: Alberta Premier Danielle Smith says with the province's neighbours to the west moving to adopt year-round daylight time, it's once again time to consider abandoning the practice of changing clocks twice a year.

items.title: $475K settlement approved in Vancouver Whitecaps class-action lawsuit
items.summary_auto: The B.C. Supreme Court has approved a $475,000 settlement in a class-action lawsuit against the Vancouver Whitecaps and Major League Soccer after star players, including Lionel Messi, were promoted to play in a 2024 game but didn't show up.

items.title: Canadians aboard tourist bus that crashed in Dominican Republic: embassy
items.summary_auto: The Embassy of Canada to the Dominican Republic says there were Canadians involved in a tourist bus accident in the Caribbean nation on Sunday night.

items.title: Did Carney go too far in offering 'support' for U.S. strikes against Iran?
items.summary_auto: Every prime minister is called upon, at one time or another, to comment on the actions of an American president. For Mark Carney, the latest moment of necessity arrived this past weekend — when the United States and Israel launched new attacks on Iran.

items.title: Iran has been cut off by internet blackouts. Those leaving give a glimpse of the reality on the ground
items.summary_auto: For the past two days, CBC News has been speaking with Iranians who have recently fleed the country, including those who were at the border, and those who had relocated to the Turkish city of Van, which is 100 kilometres away.

items.title: Child, 6, dies after sledding accident in off-island Montreal suburb
items.summary_auto: A six-year-old child has died following a sledding accident Monday afternoon near a sports centre in Chambly, Que., an off-island suburb of Montreal.

items.title: Nervous nations calling Canada's energy minister after Iran strikes
Iran conflict's jolt to oil prices could buffer Alberta's budget deficits
items.summary_auto: While oil prices stay $10 US above expectations, Alberta government rakes in an extra $20 million — every day, says an economist.

items.title: All about the Strait of Hormuz and what's at stake if the key global oil supply route is disrupted by war
items.summary_auto: Oil prices jumped Monday as the widening war in Iran disrupted tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, highlighting how important the passageway is to the world's oil supply.

items.title: U.S. House committee posts videos of Clintons answering questions about Epstein
items.summary_auto: Former U.S. president Bill Clinton and former secretary of state Hillary Clinton distanced themselves from convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein in closed-door depositions with lawmakers, according to videos that were released Monday by a House committee.

items.title: Ontario, Nova Scotia sign deal to allow direct-to-consumer alcohol sales
items.summary_auto: Producers of beer, wine and spirits will be able to start applying Tuesday to the province's liquor corporation for authorizations to do direct-to-consumer sales, a process the premiers say will only take a matter of days.

items.title: At least 153 dead after strike on girls school, Iran says
items.summary_auto: At least 153 people, including children, have been killed after a reported strike on a school in southern Iran. Iranian officials there blame the United States and Israel, while the U.S. military says it is reviewing the reports, and Israel's military says it is not aware of any IDF operations in the area.

items.title: Hilary Knight played with torn knee ligament in U.S. women's run to Olympic hockey gold
items.summary_auto: U.S. women's hockey gold medal-winning captain Hilary Knight revealed Monday in a television appearance she played in Milan with a torn medial collateral ligament in one of her knees.

items.title: Judge refuses to move transgender inmate to women’s prison ahead of judicial review
items.summary_auto: A Federal Court judge has refused to temporarily order Corrections Canada to move a transgender inmate with a history of sex offences from a men’s to a women’s prison, but has agreed the judicial review in her case should be heard more quickly.

items.title: Reactions mixed as B.C. to end time changes, adopt year-round daylight time
items.summary_auto: Reactions range from applause to concern as March 8 will be the last time most British Columbians have to change their clocks, B.C. Premier David Eby announced Monday.
